sections.heading: What Benzodiazepine Addiction Looks Like
sections.subheading: How dependence develops and why treatment becomes necessary
body: Benzodiazepines (such as Xanax, Ativan, Valium, and Klonopin) are commonly prescribed for anxiety, panic disorders, and more. While effective short-term, they can quickly lead to tolerance and dependence, even when taken as directed. Over time, the body adapts to the drug, requiring higher doses to achieve the same effect. This often leads to loss of control, increased use, and difficulty stopping without withdrawal symptoms.

Benzo addiction is not always obvious. Many individuals begin with a prescription and gradually become dependent without realizing it. When use continues despite negative effects on health or daily functioning, professional treatment is often needed. Common symptoms include:

items.title: Needing higher doses to feel the same effect
Relying on benzos to function daily

sections.heading: Why Benzodiazepine Addiction Requires Medical Detox
sections.subheading: Benzo withdrawal can be dangerous without proper supervision
body: Benzodiazepine addiction is different from many other substances because withdrawal can be severe and, in some cases, life-threatening. According to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration , withdrawal from sedatives like benzodiazepines can involve serious medical risks, including seizures, especially when not properly managed. This is why medical supervision is strongly recommended.

Unlike some substances, benzodiazepines must typically be tapered gradually under clinical supervision. Abruptly stopping or attempting to taper alone can overwhelm the nervous system and increase the risk of dangerous symptoms. Because of these risks, medical detox is often the safest and most effective first step before starting ongoing rehab.

items.text: Use of MAT when useful to manage anxiety, sleep, and discomfort

items.title: Continuous clinical assessment
items.text: To adjust care and ensure a safe transition into rehab

sections.heading: Treating the Root Causes of Benzo Addiction in Des Moines, Iowa
body: Medical detox is a critical first step for benzodiazepine addiction, but it only addresses physical dependence. Long-term recovery requires treating the underlying anxiety and mental health challenges that drive benzo use.

Once you are stable, we transition you into structured benzo rehab programs in Des Moines where the real work begins. Our focus is on helping you build coping strategies and create lasting behavioral change. We offer:

items.title: Residential Inpatient
items.text: Immersive Stabilization — Live at our private Des Moines facility while focusing fully on recovery. This structured program helps you step away from daily triggers, stabilize after detox, and begin addressing the underlying causes of benzodiazepine use.

items.title: Outpatient (PHP & IOP)
items.text: Back to Real Life — Continue treatment while living at home in Des Moines. These flexible programs provide ongoing clinical support, helping you manage anxiety, build coping skills, and maintain progress in real-world situations.

items.title: Clinical Aftercare & Alumni
items.text: Staying Sober Long-Term — Recovery from benzo addiction is ongoing. Our aftercare planning and alumni support help you stay connected, maintain stability, and continue building a life without dependence.

sections.heading: Dual Diagnosis Treatment for Benzo Addiction
sections.subheading: Integrated care for addiction and mental health
body: Benzodiazepine addiction is often closely linked to underlying mental health conditions such as anxiety, panic disorders, and trauma. Many individuals begin using medications like Xanax, Ativan, Valium, or Klonopin to manage symptoms, but over time this can lead to dependence and increased reliance.

At Des Moines Wellness Center, our dual diagnosis treatment in Des Moines addresses both benzodiazepine addiction and co-occurring mental health conditions at the same time. Treating only the substance use without addressing anxiety or other underlying issues can make long-term recovery more difficult and increase the risk of relapse. Through evidence-based therapy and clinical support, we help you develop healthier ways to manage your emotional triggers without relying on benzodiazepines.

sections.heading: Our Approach to Benzo Rehab in Des Moines, Iowa
sections.subheading: THE WAY WE HEAL benzo addiction
body: At our Des Moines center, we use a structured clinical approach designed to treat both benzodiazepine dependence and the underlying mental health conditions that drive it. Our programs combine safe gradual tapering and evidence-based therapies to help you stabilize and build long-term recovery. We offer:

items.title: Evidence-Based Therapies
items.text: We use proven approaches like CBT and DBT to help you identify triggers and replace reliance on benzodiazepines with healthier coping strategies.

items.title: Expert Clinical Staff
items.text: You’ll work with a dedicated team in Des Moines experienced in addiction treatment and mental health, providing structured and supportive care throughout recovery.

items.title: Medically Supervised Tapering
items.text: Benzo detox requires a gradual taper. Our team follows ASAM criteria to minimize withdrawal symptoms and ensure a stable detox process.

items.title: Relapse Prevention Skills
items.text: We focus on real-world strategies to manage anxiety, stress, and daily triggers without returning to benzodiazepine use.

items.title: Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)
items.text: When needed, we use clinically guided medications to support stabilization and reduce withdrawal-related discomfort when appropriate.

items.title: Individual & Group Therapy
items.text: A combination of one-on-one sessions and small group therapy helps you process challenges, build insight, and connect with others in recovery.

sections.heading: Job Protection and FMLA During Benzo Rehab
sections.subheading: Take time for treatment without risking your employment
body: Seeking treatment for benzodiazepine addiction doesn’t mean you have to put your job at risk. Many individuals qualify for protection under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) , which can allow you to take time off for medical and mental health treatment while maintaining your employment.

At Des Moines Wellness Center, our team can help guide you through the process and provide the documentation needed for FMLA when appropriate. This allows you to focus on detox and rehab without the added stress of work obligations. Taking time to address benzo dependence is an important step toward long-term stability, and job protection options can make that decision easier.

sections.heading: Benzo Rehab and Detox in Des Moines, Iowa

body: If you’re searching for benzo rehab in Des Moines or broader Iowa, finding the right level of care is critical. Benzodiazepine dependence requires a structured, medically supervised approach, especially during withdrawal, when symptoms can become severe without proper support. At Des Moines Wellness Center, we provide medical detox and full-spectrum benzo rehab in Des Moines, IA, helping individuals safely taper off benzodiazepines and transition into ongoing treatment. From initial stabilization through residential care, outpatient programs, and aftercare planning, our programs are designed to support long-term recovery at every stage.

As part of Quadrant Health Group , our care is backed by over 10 years of experience in addiction treatment across multiple facilities in the United States. This allows us to combine proven clinical standards, structured programming, and experienced staff with a personalized approach to care in Des Moines. Our team specializes in treating addiction to medications like Xanax, Ativan, Valium, and Klonopin, with a strong focus on dual diagnosis care for anxiety and related mental health conditions.

Located in Des Moines and serving communities across Iowa, our facility offers accessible care with easy access from I-35, I-80, and I-235, and close to Des Moines International Airport, making admissions and travel more convenient for both local and out-of-state clients. We accept most insurances and offer same-day admissions when necessary. If you or a loved one needs benzodiazepine addiction treatment in Des Moines, our team is here to help. Call now to speak with an admissions specialist or verify your insurance to get started.
